pattern: Turning a running timeline into consecutive gaps reduces "which press took longest" to a single linear scan with an index tie-break.
hints: Each entry's own press duration is the time since the previous entry - except the very first entry, whose duration is simply its own timestamp.
Walk the events once, keep a running "time of the previous press", and compare each computed gap against the best gap seen so far.
Track (bestIndex, bestDuration) starting from the first event; on a strictly greater duration replace both, and on an equal duration only replace if the new index is smaller.

edge_inputs.description: a single event: its own timestamp is trivially the longest (and only) duration
same button pressed twice, the second gap is the longer one
the very first event's duration equals its raw timestamp, larger than any later gap
two different buttons tie on duration; the smaller index wins
a button index is reused three times but only one of its presses has the longest single duration
minimum and maximum index/time magnitudes in a two-event sequence
a large raw index has a short duration while a small index has the longest gap

stress.description: max-length event list with strictly increasing times and random indices, occasionally repeating an index
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    t = 0
    events = []
    for _ in range(n):
        t += rng.randint(1, 90)
        idx = rng.randint(1, 100000)
        events.append([idx, t])
    return [events]
stress.note: times are strictly increasing as required by the constraints
